analysis.audience_segment: Finance & consulting professionals, Big 4 alumni, corporate LinkedIn humour consumers — extremely high insider recognition rate (23–35)

stimulating_moment.event: Tired character at laptop — 'You tell yourself the tab is almost tied out' / 'The tab is not almost tied out' — two-beat self-aware accounting joke with the highest insider specificity and deadpan delivery in the video

suggested_edits.title: Slow Branded Open
suggestions: Cut the exterior building shot and open directly at 0:03 with the 5:45am Peloton — that's the first real laugh and it lands immediately
Add a 1-frame text flash of the title over the Peloton scene instead of the static building reveal
suggested_edits.alternatives: Open with the 0:48 peak joke ('The tab is not almost tied out') as a 1s teaser, then cut to 0:03 — loop anchor that rewards completion for anyone who recognises the setup

suggested_edits.title: Dip at 0:18 and 0:36
suggestions: 0:18: The 8am desk scene is a visual reset with no new joke — cut it and let the 'circle back' line play over the subway or subway exit scene instead
0:36: 'You eat at your desk' is the weakest standalone line — pair it with a visual detail (sad salad emoji above character, phone with Outlook notification) to add a second joke layer
suggested_edits.alternatives: Restructure to alternate high and medium beats more tightly — the current two-dip pattern at 0:18 and 0:36 risks losing viewers before the strongest second half

suggested_edits.title: Hard CTA Drop at 0:57
suggestions: Replace 'Follow Stick Stories for more' text with an in-character line: 'You follow. It's on your to-do list. You will circle back to actually watching.' — keeps the voice alive through the CTA
End on the 0:54 'circling back' frame as the last visual — the walking shot with briefcase is the best visual closer; the static text outro wastes it
suggested_edits.alternatives: Add a post-CTA bonus beat (3s) with one final absurd detail, e.g. the character getting a Slack notification at midnight — gives early-departing viewers a reason to rewatch for the hidden ending
